title: Allow failures on WSL machine tests
body:
The WSL tests for `podman machine` are flaking with unreasonable frequency right now. Last Friday, I needed to re-run a job 8 times to get it to pass and complete the release of 5.7 RC1. This is simply not sustainable. Mark the tests allowed-failure until this can be resolved.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
NONE
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Can you re-run a successful job to try and generate a flake? I suppose I could force-push an additional commit with a typo fix in the README or something similar

> Can you re-run a successful job to try and generate a flake? 

Yes you can in the cirrus UI, though I doubt that would then update the Total Success job since that one will be green by that time

That said looking at the cirrus jobs the env var trick seems to work as the UI only shows optional for wsl, compare
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4685208064098304
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5811107970940928
We had another flake. Let me rerun that one and see if total success goes green...

If Total Success turns green here then I am cool merging based on the current cirrus logs it seems to behave correctly, though the test is marked "neutral" on the github UI and not red so harder for reviews to notice something is off when looking at machine PRs.

@baude: once the present PR merges, I will cherry-pick it on top of `v5.7` in a new PR and assign it to you.
